question: Compare NuScale's operating pressure (Table 1.3-1) with the RCS operating and design pressures in Ch.05 tables.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each guide is applicable, fully conformed to, or only partially conformed to, with references to supporting sections and tables for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. It relies on passive safety systems, including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems, and is housed in a steel containment partially immersed in water for effective heat removal. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods, soluble boron, or both, and integrates key components within the reactor pressure vessel to minimize external piping and enhance safety.

nuscale_ch01_0709.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.E Most Limiting Plant System Single Failure
nuscale_ch01_0709.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets regulatory criteria for various turbine and steam system failure scenarios, including loss of external load, turbine trip, and main steam isolation valve closure. It states that the design conforms to requirements for fuel cladding integrity, incident frequency, instrument setpoints, and single failure criteria, with no exceptions or additional comments noted. Additionally, the NuScale design does not include a steam pressure regulator, making that criterion not applicable.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the NuScale Power Plant’s major design features and parameters with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and system components, showing that NuScale uses significantly smaller modules, fewer fuel assemblies, and simplified systems such as no external coolant loops or pumps. The comparison is intended to illustrate the unique aspects and safety system differences of the NuScale design relative to conventional PWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0706.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.B Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0706.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets the regulatory criteria outlined in DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5 for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), specifically regarding fuel cladding integrity. It notes that NuScale does not have a steam pressure regulator and that the design conforms to requirements for incidents of moderate frequency, instrument setpoints, single failures, and performance of nonsafety-related systems, with no exceptions or additional comments.

nuscale_ch01_0557.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.8 RG 8.10
nuscale_ch01_0557.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as specified in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, and if so, whether the design fully or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.

nuscale_ch01_0705.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.A Reactor Coolant System and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0705.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various transient events affecting the reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, such as loss of external load, turbine trip, and steam pressure regulator failure. It notes that the NuScale design generally conforms to the requirements, except for partial conformance regarding the steam pressure regulator, which the NuScale design does not include. Analytical parameters and objectives for these events are addressed, with no significant deviations except as noted.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards for threaded fasteners used in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines that preservice and inservice inspection and testing of reactor coolant pressure boundary components are conducted according to the latest applicable ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) requirements, as mandated by federal regulations, with inspection intervals and code editions specified in the inspection program.

nuscale_ch01_0315.title: SRP 9.2.1, Rev 5: Station Service Water SystemII.1 Protection Against Natural Phenomena (GDC 2)
nuscale_ch01_0315.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report states that the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) is not safety-related or risk-significant and does not provide essential cooling to safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSC). The SCWS design ensures that its failure will not adversely affect safety-related SSCs, and its conformance with regulatory criteria is limited to preventing such adverse effects, with full or partial compliance to relevant General Design Criteria (GDC) as applicable.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, primarily as Class 1, and references the applicable code editions and regulatory guides used for certification.

nuscale_ch01_0316.title: SRP 9.2.1, Rev 5: Station Service Water SystemII.2 Environmental and Dynamic Effects (GDC 4)
nuscale_ch01_0316.summary: The NuScale site cooling water system (SCWS) is not safety-related or risk-significant and does not provide essential cooling to safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSC). For environmental and dynamic effects (GDC 4), the SCWS partially conforms, as its design ensures that failure of the nonsafety-related system will not adversely affect safety-related SSCs. The system's design and layout also meet requirements for sharing between units (GDC 5), with no reasonable likelihood of impacting essential safety functions.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the inspection and testing requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components and actuation valves in the NuScale reactor. It explains that these valves are exercised during cold shutdown conditions, in accordance with ASME OM Code requirements, and outlines the procedures for position verification and loss of actuator power testing. Additionally, a one-time in-situ test of the DHRS function will be performed on the first installed reactor module as part of the initial test program.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure boundary to prevent the release of fission products and provides structural support for various reactor components. Materials used for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

edges.description: Section A references regulatory guidance and conformance tables, while Section B provides technical details about the pressurizer, which is directly relevant for determining and comparing the RCS operating and design pressures as required by the analysis question.
Section B provides the NuScale operating pressure values in Table 1.3-1, which are the specific design results to be compared against the regulatory requirements or criteria referenced in Section A.

Section A provides the operating characteristics and context for NuScale's reactor pressure, which are the basis for the operating and design pressure values compared against the regulatory requirements and accident scenarios discussed in Section B.

Section A describes the general operating characteristics of the NuScale module, including its reliance on natural circulation and absence of certain penetrations, while Section B details the pressurizer's design as part of the reactor vessel, both of which are relevant to understanding and comparing the RCS operating and design pressures referenced in their respective tables.

Section B's Table 1.3-1 provides NuScale's nominal operating pressure values, which can be directly compared to the RCS operating and design pressures referenced in Section A's general operating characteristics.

Section A provides the operating characteristics and context for NuScale's reactor coolant system, which are necessary to interpret the RCS operating and design pressures referenced in Section B's regulatory conformance tables.

Section A references the regulatory requirements for system pressures and fuel cladding integrity during transients, while Section B provides the design details of the pressurizer, which directly determines the RCS operating and design pressures that must be compared to the requirements in Section A.

Section B provides the nominal operating pressure values for NuScale and typical PWRs (Table 1.3-1), which are the specific design parameters that must be compared against the RCS operating and design pressures referenced in Section A's regulatory conformance analysis.
Section A references Section B by summarizing the conformance of the NuScale design with regulatory criteria, specifically citing the fuel cladding integrity requirements detailed in Section B.

Section B's Table 1.3-1 provides the nominal NuScale operating pressure, which can be directly compared to the RCS operating and design pressures detailed in Section A's Ch.05 tables to assess compliance or differences.

Section A describes the pressurizer's role in maintaining and communicating RCS pressure, which is directly relevant to the operating and design pressures compared in Section B's regulatory conformance context.

Section A provides the nominal operating pressure values for NuScale in Table 1.3-1, which are the basis for comparison with the RCS operating and design pressures found in the Chapter 5 tables referenced in the analysis question.

Section A and Section B both document NuScale FSAR conformance with regulatory criteria, but Section A addresses radiation protection design features while Section B addresses system response and fuel cladding integrity under transient conditions, with both sections referencing compliance tables (e.g., Table 1.9-2) that connect their regulatory evaluations.

Section A references the regulatory criteria and analytical parameters for RCS pressures, while Section B provides the actual operating characteristics and design details (including pressures) of the NuScale system, allowing a direct comparison of design values to regulatory requirements.
Section A provides the analytical parameters, including RCS operating pressure values, that are compared against the fuel cladding integrity requirements and system limits discussed in Section B.

Section A describes the RCS's pressure-retaining functions and degasification features, while Section B outlines the NPM's operating characteristics, both of which are connected through their implications for the system's operating and design pressures as referenced in the analysis question.

Section A describes the RCS pressure-retaining features relevant to operating pressure, while Section B references regulatory criteria that require conformance of RCS operating and design pressures, connecting them through the need to demonstrate that design values meet regulatory requirements.

Section B describes the design and function of the RCS, including its pressure-retaining features, which are the subject of the operating and design pressures to be compared with the values listed in Section A's referenced tables.

Section A references the analytical parameters and regulatory conformance for RCS pressures, while Section B provides the system description and functions of the RCS, connecting them through the design and operational context of RCS pressure values.

Section A specifies the material and design requirements for threaded fasteners in the RCS, which must withstand the operating pressures described in Section B's summary of NPM operating characteristics, thus linking fastener integrity to the stated RCS operating conditions.

Section A provides material and design details for RCS pressure boundary components, which are relevant to evaluating whether the operating and design pressures (as compared in Section B's referenced tables) are within the allowable limits for those components.

Section A states that the NuScale reactor pressure vessel and pressure-retaining components are designed to meet ASME BPVC Class 1 requirements per 10 CFR 50.55a, while Section B describes the operating characteristics of the NuScale Power Module, including its use of natural circulation and passive safety systems, both of which are relevant to the pressure and integrity requirements referenced in Section A.

Section A establishes that the NuScale reactor coolant pressure boundary is designed and constructed to meet ASME Code requirements, which set allowable pressure limits, while Section B discusses the plant's conformance with regulatory criteria during operational transients that could challenge those pressure limits, thus connecting the design basis (A) to the operational performance and safety analysis (B).

Section A describes the materials and standards for threaded fasteners in the RCS, specifying compliance with ASME BPVC Sections II and III, while Section B establishes that all RCPB components, including those in Section A, must comply with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC Section III, thus Section A provides specific implementation details for the general regulatory compliance stated in Section B.

Section A discusses the applicability of GDC 2 to the site cooling water system (SCWS) and its lack of safety-related functions, while Section B details compliance of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) with ASME and regulatory codes, connecting them through the broader context of regulatory conformance for different plant systems.

Section A and Section B both address the NuScale site cooling water system's (SCWS) conformance with different General Design Criteria (GDC 2 and GDC 4), and both specify that the SCWS is not safety-related, focusing on ensuring its failure does not adversely affect safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSCs).

Section A describes the reactor vessel as a pressure-retaining component of the reactor coolant system (RCS), while Section B outlines the operating characteristics of the NuScale Power Module, both of which provide context for understanding and comparing the operating and design pressures referenced in the analysis question.

Section A describes the physical configuration and pressure-retaining function of the reactor vessel as part of the RCS, while Section B references regulatory criteria and accident scenarios that the RCS (including the reactor vessel) must withstand, connecting them through the requirement that the vessel's design and operating pressures must satisfy the regulatory limits for accident conditions.
